name: pyproject-metadata
documentation:
# pyproject-metadata

[![pre-commit.ci status][pre-commit-badge]][pre-commit-link]
[![checks][gha-checks-badge]][gha-checks-link]
[![tests][gha-tests-badge]][gha-tests-link]
[![codecov][codecov-badge]][codecov-link]
[![Documentation Status][rtd-badge]][rtd-link]
[![PyPI version][pypi-version]][pypi-link]

> Dataclass for PEP 621 metadata with support for [core metadata] generation

This project does not implement the parsing of `pyproject.toml` containing PEP
621 metadata.

Instead, given a Python data structure representing PEP 621 metadata (already
parsed), it will validate this input and generate a PEP 643-compliant metadata
file (e.g. `PKG-INFO`).

## Usage

After
[installing `pyproject-metadata`](https://pypi.org/project/pyproject-metadata/),
you can use it as a library in your scripts and programs:

```python
from pyproject_metadata import StandardMetadata

parsed_pyproject = {...}  # you can use parsers like `tomli` to obtain this dict
metadata = StandardMetadata.from_pyproject(parsed_pyproject, allow_extra_keys=False)
print(metadata.entrypoints)  # same fields as defined in PEP 621

pkg_info = metadata.as_rfc822()
print(str(pkg_info))  # core metadata
```

## SPDX licenses (METADATA 2.4+)

If `project.license` is a string or `project.license-files` is present, then
METADATA 2.4+ will be used. A user is expected to validate and normalize
`metadata.license` with an SPDX validation tool, such as the one being added to
`packaging`. Add something like this:

```python
if isinstance(metadata.license, str):
    metadata.license = packaging.licenses.normalize_license_expression(metadata.license)
```

A backend is also expected to copy entries from `project.licence_files`, which
are paths relative to the project directory, into the `dist-info/licenses`
folder, preserving the original source structure.

## Dynamic Metadata (METADATA 2.2+)

Pyproject-metadata supports dynamic metadata. To use it, specify your METADATA
fields in `dynamic_metadata`. If you want to convert `pyproject.toml` field
names to METADATA field(s), use
`pyproject_metadata.pyproject_to_metadata("field-name")`, which will return a
frozenset of metadata names that are touched by that field.

## Adding extra fields

You can add extra fields to the Message returned by `to_rfc822()`, as long as
they are valid metadata entries.

## Collecting multiple errors

You can use the `all_errors` argument to `from_pyproject` to show all errors in
the metadata parse at once, instead of raising an exception on the first one.
The exception type will be `pyproject_metadata.errors.ExceptionGroup` (which is
just `ExceptionGroup` on Python 3.11+).

## Validating extra fields

By default, a warning (`pyproject_metadata.errors.ExtraKeyWarning`) will be
issued for extra fields at the project table. You can pass `allow_extra_keys=`
to either avoid the check (`True`) or hard error (`False`). If you want to
detect extra keys, you can get them with `pyproject_metadata.extra_top_level`
and `pyproject_metadata.extra_build_system`. It is recommended that build
systems only warn on failures with these extra keys.

## Validating classifiers

If you want to validate classifiers, then install the `trove_classifiers`
library (the canonical source for classifiers), and run:

```python
import trove_classifiers

metadata_classifieres = {
    c for c in metadata.classifiers if not c.startswith("Private ::")
}
invalid_classifiers = set(metadata.classifiers) - trove_classifiers.classifiers

# Also the deprecated dict if you want it
dep_names = set(metadata.classifiers) & set(trove_classifiers.deprecated_classifiers)
deprecated_classifiers = {
    k: trove_classifiers.deprecated_classifiers[k] for k in dep_names
}
```

If you are writing a build backend, you should not validate classifiers with a
`Private ::` prefix; these are only restricted for upload to PyPI (such as
`Private :: Do Not Upload`).

Since classifiers are a moving target, it is probably best for build backends
(which may be shipped by third party distributors like Debian or Fedora) to
either ignore or have optional classifier validation.

modules.name: pyproject_metadata.constants
documentation:
Constants for the pyproject_metadata package, collected here to make them easy
to update. These should be considered mostly private.
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

modules.name: pyproject_metadata.errors
documentation:
This module defines exceptions and error handling utilities. It is the
recommend path to access `ConfiguratonError`, `ConfigurationWarning`, and
`ExceptionGroup`. For backward compatibility, `ConfigurationError` is
re-exported in the top-level package.
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

classes.name: pyproject_metadata.errors.ConfigurationError
documentation:
Error in the backend metadata. Has an optional key attribute, which will be non-None
if the error is related to a single key in the pyproject.toml file.

classes.name: pyproject_metadata.errors.ConfigurationWarning
documentation: Warnings about backend metadata.

classes.name: pyproject_metadata.errors.ExceptionGroup
documentation:
A minimal implementation of `ExceptionGroup` from Python 3.11.

Users can replace this with a more complete implementation, such as from
the exceptiongroup backport package, if better error messages and
integration with tooling is desired and the addition of a dependency is
acceptable.

classes.name: pyproject_metadata.errors.ErrorCollector

methods.name: pyproject_metadata.errors.ErrorCollector.config_error
documentation: Raise a configuration error, or add it to the error list.

methods.name: pyproject_metadata.errors.ErrorCollector.finalize
documentation: Raise a group exception if there are any errors.

methods.name: pyproject_metadata.errors.ErrorCollector.collect
documentation: Support nesting; add any grouped errors to the error list.

Collect errors and raise them as a group at the end (if collect_errors is True),
otherwise raise them immediately.

modules.name: pyproject_metadata.project_table
documentation:
This module contains type definitions for the tables used in the
`pyproject.toml`.  You should either import this at type-check time only, or
make sure `typing_extensions` is available for Python 3.10 and below.

Documentation notice: the fields with hyphens are not shown due to a sphinx-autodoc bug.
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

modules.name: pyproject_metadata.pyproject
documentation:
This module focues on reading pyproject.toml fields with error collection. It is
mostly internal, except for License and Readme classes, which are re-exported in
the top-level package.
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

classes.name: pyproject_metadata.pyproject.License
documentation:
This represents a classic license, which contains text, and optionally a
file path. Modern licenses are just SPDX identifiers, which are strings.

classes.name: pyproject_metadata.pyproject.Readme
documentation:
This represents a readme, which contains text and a content type, and
optionally a file path.

classes.name: pyproject_metadata.pyproject.PyProjectReader

methods.name: pyproject_metadata.pyproject.PyProjectReader.ensure_str
documentation: Ensure that a value is a string.

methods.name: pyproject_metadata.pyproject.PyProjectReader.ensure_list
documentation: Ensure that a value is a list of strings.

methods.name: pyproject_metadata.pyproject.PyProjectReader.ensure_dict
documentation: Ensure that a value is a dictionary of strings.

methods.name: pyproject_metadata.pyproject.PyProjectReader.ensure_people
documentation: Ensure that a value is a list of tables with optional "name" and "email" keys.

methods.name: pyproject_metadata.pyproject.PyProjectReader.get_license
documentation:
Get the license field from the project table. Handles PEP 639 style license too.

None is returned if the license field is not present or if an error occurred.

methods.name: pyproject_metadata.pyproject.PyProjectReader.get_license_files
documentation:
Get the license-files list of files from the project table.

Returns None if an error occurred (including invalid globs, etc) or if
not present.

methods.name: pyproject_metadata.pyproject.PyProjectReader.get_readme
documentation:
Get the text of the readme from the project table.

Returns None if an error occurred or if the readme field is not present.

methods.name: pyproject_metadata.pyproject.PyProjectReader.get_dependencies
documentation: Get the dependencies from the project table.

methods.name: pyproject_metadata.pyproject.PyProjectReader.get_optional_dependencies
documentation: Get the optional dependencies from the project table.

methods.name: pyproject_metadata.pyproject.PyProjectReader.get_entrypoints
documentation: Get the entrypoints from the project table.

methods.name: pyproject_metadata.pyproject.PyProjectReader.get_dynamic
documentation:
Get the dynamic fields from the project table.

Returns an empty list if the field is not present or if an error occurred.

methods.name: pyproject_metadata.pyproject.PyProjectReader._get_files_from_globs
documentation: Given a list of globs, get files that match.

Class for reading pyproject.toml fields with error collection.

Unrelated errors are collected and raised at once if the `collect_errors`
parameter is set to `True`. Some methods will return None if an error was
raised. Most of them expect a non-None value as input to enforce the caller
to handle missing vs. error correctly. The exact design is based on usage,
as this is an internal class.

modules.name: pyproject_metadata
documentation:
This is pyproject_metadata, a library for working with PEP 621 metadata.

Example usage:

```python
from pyproject_metadata import StandardMetadata

metadata = StandardMetadata.from_pyproject(
    parsed_pyproject, allow_extra_keys=False, all_errors=True, metadata_version="2.3"
)

pkg_info = metadata.as_rfc822()
with open("METADATA", "wb") as f:
    f.write(pkg_info.as_bytes())

ep = self.metadata.entrypoints.copy()
ep["console_scripts"] = self.metadata.scripts
ep["gui_scripts"] = self.metadata.gui_scripts
for group, entries in ep.items():
    if entries:
        with open("entry_points.txt", "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
            print(f"[{group}]", file=f)
            for name, target in entries.items():
                print(f"{name} = {target}", file=f)
            print(file=f)
```
SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

functions.name: pyproject_metadata.field_to_metadata
documentation: Return the METADATA fields that correspond to a project field.

functions.name: pyproject_metadata.extras_top_level
documentation: Return any extra keys in the top-level of the pyproject table.

functions.name: pyproject_metadata.extras_build_system
documentation: Return any extra keys in the build-system table.

functions.name: pyproject_metadata.extras_project
documentation: Return any extra keys in the project table.

functions.name: pyproject_metadata._name_list
documentation: Build a comma-separated list of names.

functions.name: pyproject_metadata._email_list
documentation: Build a comma-separated list of emails.

functions.name: pyproject_metadata._build_extra_req
documentation: Build a new requirement with an extra marker.

classes.name: pyproject_metadata._SmartMessageSetter
documentation:
This provides a nice internal API for setting values in an Message to
reduce boilerplate.

If a value is None, do nothing.

classes.name: pyproject_metadata._JSonMessageSetter
documentation:
This provides an API to build a JSON message output in the same way as the
classic Message. Line breaks are preserved this way.

classes.name: pyproject_metadata.RFC822Policy
documentation:
This is {class}`email.policy.EmailPolicy`, but with a simple `header_store_parse`
implementation that handles multiline values, and some nice defaults.

classes.name: pyproject_metadata.RFC822Message

methods.name: pyproject_metadata.RFC822Message.as_bytes
documentation: This handles unicode encoding.

This is {class}`email.message.EmailMessage` with two small changes: it defaults to
our `RFC822Policy`, and it correctly writes unicode when being called
with `bytes()`.

classes.name: pyproject_metadata.StandardMetadata

methods.name: pyproject_metadata.StandardMetadata.auto_metadata_version
documentation:
This computes the metadata version based on the fields set in the object
if `metadata_version` is None.

methods.name: pyproject_metadata.StandardMetadata.canonical_name
documentation: Return the canonical name of the project.

methods.name: pyproject_metadata.StandardMetadata.from_pyproject
documentation:
Read metadata from a pyproject.toml table. This is the main method for
creating an instance of this class. It also supports two additional
fields: `allow_extra_keys` to control what happens when extra keys are
present in the pyproject table, and `all_errors`, to  raise all errors
in an ExceptionGroup instead of raising the first one.

methods.name: pyproject_metadata.StandardMetadata.as_rfc822
documentation: Return an RFC822 message with the metadata.

methods.name: pyproject_metadata.StandardMetadata.as_json
documentation: Return a JSON message with the metadata.

methods.name: pyproject_metadata.StandardMetadata.validate
documentation:
Validate metadata for consistency and correctness. Will also produce
warnings if `warn` is given. Respects `all_errors`. This is called
when loading a pyproject.toml, and when making metadata. Checks:

- `metadata_version` is a known version or None
- `name` is a valid project name
- `license_files` can't be used with classic `license`
- License classifiers can't be used with SPDX license
- `description` is a single line (warning)
- `license` is not an SPDX license expression if metadata_version >= 2.4 (warning)
- License classifiers deprecated for metadata_version >= 2.4 (warning)
- `license` is an SPDX license expression if metadata_version >= 2.4
- `license_files` is supported only for metadata_version >= 2.4
- `project_url` can't contain keys over 32 characters

methods.name: pyproject_metadata.StandardMetadata._write_metadata
documentation: Write the metadata to the message. Handles JSON or Message.

class_variables.name: pyproject_metadata.StandardMetadata.dynamic
documentation: This field is used to track dynamic fields. You can't set a field not in this list.

class_variables.name: pyproject_metadata.StandardMetadata.dynamic_metadata
documentation: This is a list of METADATA fields that can change in between SDist and wheel. Requires metadata_version 2.2+.

class_variables.name: pyproject_metadata.StandardMetadata.metadata_version
documentation: This is the target metadata version. If None, it will be computed as a minimum based on the fields set.

class_variables.name: pyproject_metadata.StandardMetadata.all_errors
documentation: If True, all errors will be collected and raised in an ExceptionGroup.

This class represents the standard metadata fields for a project. It can be
used to read metadata from a pyproject.toml table, validate it, and write it
to an RFC822 message or JSON.
